subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize Elm Review in this directory

options.description: Show help for elm-review init
Create the configuration files in the specified directory instead of in the review/ directory
Copy the review configuration from a GitHub repository, at the root or in a folder
Specify the path to the elm compiler

subcommands.name: suppress
subcommands.description: Generate suppression files for rules that report many errors

options.description: Show help for elm-review suppress
Checks whether there are uncommitted suppression files
Include suppressed errors in the error report for all rules
Include suppressed errors in the error report for all rules

subcommands.name: new-package
subcommands.description: Creates an new project aimed to contain rules and to be published later

options.description: Show help for elm-review new-package
Specify the path to the elm compiler

subcommands.name: new-rule
subcommands.description: Adds a new rule to your review configuration or review package

options.description: Show help for elm-review new-package

args.name: rule name
args.description: Add a rule file in src/ and a corresponding test file in tests/. Should be formatted just like an Elm module name, such as NoDoingThis or No.Doing.This

options.description: Show help for elm-review
Include suppressed errors in the error report for all rules
Run with a subsection of the rules in the configuration. Specify them by their name, and separate them by commas
Re-run elm-review automatically when your project or configuration changes
Re-run elm-review automatically when your project changes
Specify the path to the elm.json file of the project
Use the review configuration in the specified directory
Use the review configuration from a GitHub repository
Specify the path to the elm compiler
Print the version of the elm-review CLI
Add helpful information to debug your configuration or rules
Error reports will be in JSON format
Hide the details from error reports for a more compact view
Ignore the reports of all rules for the specified directories
Ignore the reports of all rules for the specified files
Elm Review will present fixes for the errors that offer an automatic fix, which you can then accept or refuse one by one
Elm Review will present a single fix containing the application of all available automatic fixes, which you can then accept or refuse
Specify the path to elm-format
Limit the number of fixes applied in a single batch to N

Enable extracting data from the project for the rules that have a data extractor. Requires running with --report=json. Learn more by reading the section about "Extracting information" at https://bit.l
Print out how much time it took for rules and phases of the process to run. This is meant for benchmarking purposes
Disable colors in the output
